transcript.text: So Belgrade is supposed to be the party capital of Europe.
That's what some chick said.
And then I looked in like the Lonely Planet on the internet.
Yeah, it's supposed to be the party capital of Europe.
But like, yeah, they have these beers.
These like three liter bottles of beers for like less than two freaking dollars.
So I basically brought my bottle of beer around with me all over Belgrade.
Did the tour, drinking it.
That's kind of when I started.
Because I was drinking beer going around Vienna.
Because it's legal to drink in public, drink beer in public in Europe.
And as an American, that was a cool experience to do.
And yeah, so and the girls who worked there, like every night it was a different girl.
And I was the only person there the entire time except for the first night.
But I wasn't really hanging out with the chicks.
They were kind of addicted to the TV, just watching the TV.
But they were hot.
My God, every one of them was like, bam.
One of them was like, whoa.
Serbian chicks are fucking hot.
Because they've got like, now I mentioned this in my Serbian video.
And it pissed some Serbians off big time.
But Serbians have darker skin than the Croatians and all those other Europeans.
I'm sorry, but they do.
And that's what makes those chicks hot.
Their skin is olive colored.
And it's fucking hot.
And they're tall.
So they're hot.
This chick who was really hot in the hostel who was working there wasn't tall, but she was hot.
Oh yeah, I was talking to some Serbian dude and he was all like, really interested in me, talking to me.
Well, he was talking to some dude asking to do a survey working in the streets or something like that.
And then, well, I stayed the fourth night there because it was a full moon and I wanted to try to do a 24-hour long bike ride.
But I had like four Big Mac meals, two that morning and two the night before, and I got sick.
And I also had been drinking out of water bottles that had juice stored in them for days on end and had this like black fuzz on the inside of it that I couldn't get out.
And I was drinking out of that and I got sick and maybe that could have been why.
So I rolled in and there was this really beautiful like campground area that had cabins next to a river that was actually the border with Bosnia that I stopped at and I stayed in, had my own cabin.
And like they were extremely interested in me there.
They interviewed me for a newspaper and then they gave me this huge meal for free, delicious meal saying, you know, with this long note, oh, I hope your bike tour goes well.
You know, please accept this as a token of Serbian hospitality is really nice.
And it was a place where Robert De Niro had stayed for a few days for like a week or something back when he was like 18 hitchhiking.
And like right in front of my hot my cabin, it was like called the Robert De Niro cabin.
It was written.
It's kind of funny.
So then I rode into Bosnia and in one day, I think, because I don't remember camping any place in between into Sarajevo.
And I went up two big passes and I was up one pass and it was kind of late.
It was like seven.
I was like, oh, man, it's getting kind of late.
Why did I get started so late?
I don't have to ride through the darkness.
I wanted to make it to Sarajevo today.
And it went down and it was downhill, downhill, downhill, downhill.
Fifteen minutes, 20 minutes later, I was in Sarajevo.
Downhill all the way to Sarajevo.
Right when I entered Sarajevo through the tunnel, boom, there's historical downtown center of Sarajevo.
So Sarajevo is like the capital, but it's kind of like you can get the impression it's a tiny little town.
Because it's like you're nowhere land, you go through a tunnel, boom, you're in the center of downtown central Sarajevo.
I tried to look at the tunnel, but I didn't.
They were under siege for four years back in 97 by the Serbian Bosnians, shelling them for four years.
And the only way to get supplies was through this tiny little tunnel.
Now, that if you stood up, you'd get electrocuted to death because the electricity came through there too.
And I don't know how they were managed to keep that tunnel active with the Bosnians, or the Serbian Bosnians, shelling them.
But I didn't get to see it.
I looked out and I couldn't find it.
And nobody around could find it and knew where it was.
So finally, I just, yeah, I asked these military people, some hot butch chick with a machine gun.
With all these other guys, I was like, oh, I don't know.
So, but I was there three nights.
Because one day I stayed there the entire day watching all the episodes of Pacific at the computer place.
I took a little, another little vacation from my vacation.
Four nights, unnecessary nights in Belgrade, and then three in Sarajevo.
And then I rode out south from Sarajevo.

Beautiful.
